title: R-cut: Delete externalFixturePath schema field, test fixtures, and any reader sites
description: Apply the deletion checklist from TASK-003. Remove the `externalFixturePath: z.string().optional(),` line from fabric-config.ts schema. Remove the field from all 3 test fixtures (fabric-config.test.ts:106+115, schemas-roundtrip.test.ts:50, zod-roundtrip.test.ts:47). Delete any production reader code (TASK-003 expected zero, confirm). Add a `BREAKING:` note to CHANGELOG.md under `## [2.0.0-rc.17]` documenting the field removal and pointing users to `EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH` env var. Pre-user clean-slate: NO migration shim, NO z.preprocess alias — pre-rc.17 fabric-config.json with the field will fail parse with a clear `Unrecognized key` error (acceptable per memory/feedback_clean_slate.md).

files.change: Delete the line — schema becomes strict on the removed key
Remove externalFixturePath fixture entry and matching expect assertion
Add BREAKING note: 'Removed `externalFixturePath` from fabric.config.json schema. Use `EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH` env var for dev/test fixture paths. No migration shim.'

implementation: Apply schema deletion: remove line 50 from fabric-config.ts (verify no surrounding context breaks)
Update fabric-config.test.ts: drop lines 106 (fixture) + 115 (expect) and adjust any test name if it referenced the field
Update schemas-roundtrip.test.ts:50: remove the externalFixturePath property from the fixture object
Update zod-roundtrip.test.ts:47: remove the externalFixturePath line from the fc.record arbitrary
Add CHANGELOG entry under `## [2.0.0-rc.17]` ➜ `### BREAKING` subsection
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test` — must pass
Post-delete sweep: `grep -rn 'externalFixturePath' packages/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v dist | grep -v coverage` MUST return empty

Add a negative test: a fabric.config.json containing `externalFixturePath` should fail parse with `Unrecognized key` (since clientPathsSchema uses .strict() but fabricConfigSchema does not — confirm whether to add .strict() here too or accept silent ignore)

reference.pattern: rc.12 hard-rename of knowledgeLanguageSchema (no preprocess alias)
reference.examples: See lines 33-40 of fabric-config.ts where rc.12 documents `no z.preprocess alias` — mirror that comment style for rc.17 deletion

criteria: `grep -rn 'externalFixturePath' packages/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v dist | grep -v coverage` returns empty (zero matches)
Test command: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test` passes (all 3 affected test files green)
CHANGELOG.md `## [2.0.0-rc.17]` section contains a `### BREAKING` bullet about externalFixturePath removal
Verify env var is preserved: `grep -rn 'EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH' packages/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v dist | grep -v coverage` still returns reader sites (i18n descriptions, env-var consumers)

Behavioral expectation: a fabric.config.json with the deleted field either parses-and-ignores (current default) or fails with a clear `Unrecognized key` error — document which in the negative-test result

rationale.chosen_approach: Mechanical deletion driven by TASK-003's checklist; no shim, no soft-deprecation — pure clean-slate
alternatives_considered: Add z.preprocess to silently strip the field from old configs
Mark deprecated with a parse-time warning for one RC then delete
decision_factors: Pre-user clean-slate (Fabric has zero users) — no migration burden (per memory/feedback_clean_slate.md)
rc.12 set the precedent of hard-renames with no shim — rc.17 follows same convention
rationale.tradeoffs: Hard-break for any local dev still using the field; mitigated by CHANGELOG note + env var alternative

manual_checks: Construct a fabric.config.json with `externalFixturePath` set, run any command that loads it, verify error or silent-ignore behavior matches CHANGELOG promise
Confirm EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH env var still resolves correctly in dev/test scenarios
